name: Richard Hayman
abstract: Richard Warren Joseph Hayman (March 27, 1920 – February 5, 2014) was an American musician who was the chief music arranger of the Boston Pops Orchestra for over 50 years, and served as a pops conductor for orchestras including the Detroit Symphony Orchestra, the St. Louis Symphony and the Grand Rapids Symphony in Grand Rapids, Michigan.

works.t: Faust: Act II. Waltz, "Vin ou Bière"

concerts.label: Benefit: Shield Institute for Retarded Children
The New York Pops 10th Birthday Gala Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Charles Calello
about_quotes.evidence: Richard Hayman, who I became good friends with and became the assistant conductor of the orchestra here in Boca Raton that he had for many years.
about_quotes.doc_title: Charles Calello interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, July 21, 2025
